title: Executive Order 9505—Authorizing the Secretary of War to Take Possession of and Operate the Plants and Facilities of Cudahy Brothers Company of Cudahy, Wisconsin

flags.justification: Broad seizure and operating authority ('any manner that he deems necessary'), narrowed somewhat by the requirement to observe a specific National War Labor Board directive order (Sec. 2).
Sec. 4 sets a concrete 60-day cap, and Sec. 2 ties operation to a specific, named directive order.
Single purpose: seizure of this one company's plants.
Sec. 2 requires observing a specific, named War Labor Board directive order.
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
Sec. 4's 60-day cap is a genuine, explicit termination trigger.
No judicial-process-blocking or similar oversight-removal language is present.
The 60-day cap and the requirement to follow a specific pre-existing War Labor Board determination provide real checks.

This order is triggered by a 'threatened' rather than an actual interruption of operations — a lower activation threshold than most sibling seizure orders in this corpus, which require an existing disturbance. Expanding the trigger condition to threatened-but-not-yet-realized disruption is a meaningful precedent for how broadly this authority can be invoked.
No other agency's mandate is implicated; no conflict is created.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 2/20 = 10.0%. Distinguished from most sibling seizure orders by acting on a merely 'threatened' rather than actual interruption.
